response_constraints: Do not claim the cancellation succeeded from the timeout alone.
After the timeout, retrieve the exact target ID before deciding whether to retry.
Retry cancellation only when verification returns status active; never retry a cancelled or cancellation-pending record.
Do not call search, create, or notify tools, and do not touch the distractor.
In the final response: identify the target first, state verified final status second, and report retry/no-retry last.

arguments.reason: no longer needed for the scheduled work

stages.purpose: Submit the requested cancellation once; the injected timeout leaves commit status uncertain.
In a later assistant action, verify the exact target before deciding whether a retry is safe.
Retry once only because stage 2 established that the first attempt did not commit.

state_assertions: The first crm_cancel call times out with commit status unknown.
Verification returns active, so crm_cancel is called exactly one additional time.
Distractor crm-569 remains active and unchanged.

prompt: Cancel the account “Arbor Foods renewal” (ID crm-169) with reason “no longer needed for the scheduled work.” The first cancel call may time out after reaching the service. Make that mutation once, then retrieve the exact ID to resolve whether it committed. Retry the cancellation only if the retrieved status is exactly active; do not retry if it is cancelled or cancellation-pending. Do not search for or touch the similarly named distractor. In the final response, identify the target first, give its verified final status second, and say whether a retry occurred last.
